root_cause.text: The application uses a non-constant-time byte array comparison (`Arrays.equals`) to verify cryptographic MAC signatures, making the validation process vulnerable to timing attacks.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces `Arrays.equals(actualAuthTag, expectedAuthTag)` and similar checks with `MessageDigest.isEqual(...)`, which provides a constant-time comparison that prevents timing leaks.

agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces `Arrays.equals(actualAuthTag, expectedAuthTag)` and similar checks with `MessageDigest.isEqual(...)`, which provides a constant-time comparison that prevents timing leaks.

intent_record.intent_summary: Replace Arrays.equals with MessageDigest.isEqual to prevent timing attacks during MAC signature validation.

relevance_review.reason: The group updates the imports by adding MessageDigest and removing Arrays, which directly supports the replacement of the vulnerable Arrays.equals with the constant-time MessageDigest.isEqual to mitigate the timing attack.

The group directly replaces non-constant-time array comparisons with constant-time comparisons (e.g., MessageDigest.isEqual) to prevent timing attacks during MAC signature validation, exactly addressing the root cause.

Author: Sergey Beryozkin <sberyozkin@talend.com>

diff --git a/rt/rs/security/oauth-parent/oauth2/src/main/java/org/apache/cxf/rs/security/oauth2/tokens/hawk/AbstractHawkAccessTokenValidator.java b/rt/rs/security/oauth-parent/oauth2/src/main/java/org/apache/cxf/rs/security/oauth2/tokens/hawk/AbstractHawkAccessTokenValida
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `validateAccessToken` explicitly uses the non-constant-time `Arrays.equals(serverMacData, clientMacData)` to verify the MAC signature, which matches the vulnerability root cause. A constant-time comparison is not present, so the code remains vulnerable.
